sleep 10
echo "=== POST /api/tickets/classify ==="
curl -s http://localhost:5199/api/tickets/classify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subject":"Double charged","description":"I was billed twice this month","priority":2}' | python3 -m json.tool

curl -s http://localhost:5199/api/tickets/classify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subject":"App crashes","description":"The application crashes on startup with error 500","priority":1}' | python3 -m json.tool
kill $SERVER_PID 2>/dev/null

data.content:
Everything is working. Here's what was implemented:

**Files created/modified:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `TicketClassifier.csproj` | Added `Microsoft.ML 4.0.2` + CSV copy-to-output |
| `Models/TicketData.cs` | ML.NET input/output schema with `LoadColumn` attributes |
| `Models/TicketRequest.cs` | API request/response DTOs |
| `Models/EvaluationMetrics.cs` | Metrics DTOs (accuracy, precision, recall per class) |
| `Services/TicketClassifierService.cs` | Trains model at startup using SDCA Maximum Entropy; featurizes Subject + Description text + Priority; evaluates on training data |
| `Controllers/TicketsController.cs` | `POST /api/tickets/classify` — predicts category with confidence scores; `GET /api/tickets/metrics` — returns evaluation metrics |
| `Program.cs` | Registers classifier as singleton |

**Pipeline:** Text featurization (n-grams) on Subject & Description → concatenated with Priority → SdcaMaximumEntropy multiclass trainer.

**Endpoints:**
- **`POST /api/tickets/classify`** — returns `{ predictedCategory, confidence: { Billing, General, Technical } }`
- **`GET /api/tickets/metrics`** — returns macro/micro accuracy, log loss, and per-class precision/recall

> ⚠️ The 100% training metrics are expected with only 6 samples evaluated on themselves. With a larger dataset you'd want cross-validation or a train/test split for realistic metrics.
